# Kasper.js — AI Reference

Kasper.js is a lightweight component framework with fine-grained signal-based reactivity. 
No virtual DOM. Templates are valid HTML with `@` prefixed directives. Expressions use `{{ }}`. Zero runtime dependencies. Single-file components (`.kasper`) require the `vite-plugin-kasper` Vite plugin.

Full docs: https://kasperjs.top
npm: kasper-js
Vite plugin: vite-plugin-kasper

---

## Scaffold a new project

```bash
npm create kasper-app@latest my-project
cd my-project
npm install
npm run dev
```

This generates a ready-to-use Vite project with the following structure:

```
my-project/
  index.html
  vite.config.ts
  src/
    main.ts
    kasper.d.ts
    components/
      App.kasper
      Counter.kasper
      Example.kasper
```

**`vite.config.ts`**
```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import kasper from 'vite-plugin-kasper';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [kasper()],
});
```

**`src/kasper.d.ts`** — required for TypeScript to recognise `.kasper` imports:
```ts
declare module '*.kasper' {
  import type { Component } from 'kasper-js';
  type AnyComponent = new (...args: any[]) => Component;
  const exports: Record<string, AnyComponent>;
  export = exports;
}
```

**`src/main.ts`**
```ts
import { App as KasperApp } from './components/App.kasper';
import { Example } from './components/Example.kasper';
import { App } from 'kasper-js';

App({
  root: document.querySelector<HTMLElement>('#app')!,
  entry: 'app',
  registry: {
    app: { component: KasperApp },
    example: { component: Example },
  },
});
```

Every component used as a tag in templates must be registered in the `registry`. 
The registry key is the HTML tag name used in templates.

---

## Bootstrap

```ts
import { App } from 'kasper-js';
import { MyApp } from './App.kasper';

App({
  root: document.querySelector('#app'),
  entry: 'my-app',
  registry: {
    'my-app': { component: MyApp },
  },
});
```

---

## Single-file component (.kasper)

```html
<!-- Counter.kasper -->
<template>
  <div>
    <p>{{count.value}}</p>
    <button @on:click="increment()">+</button>
  </div>
</template>

<script>
import { Component, signal } from 'kasper-js';

export class Counter extends Component {
  count = signal(0);
  increment() { this.count.value++; }
}
</script>

<style>
/* injected globally into <head> */
</style>
```

The Vite plugin attaches `Counter.template` and `Counter.$imports` automatically. 
All imports in the script block are available in the template.

---

## Component class

```ts
import { Component, signal, computed } from 'kasper-js';

export class MyComponent extends Component {
  // reactive state
  count = signal(0);
  double = computed(() => this.count.value * 2);

  // component args passed via @: attributes from parent
  get title() { return this.args.title ?? 'Default'; }

  // lifecycle
  onMount()   {}  // once, after first render — DOM ready, args populated
  onRender()  {}  // after every render cycle
  onChanges() {}  // before each reactive re-render (not first mount)
  onDestroy() {}  // when removed from DOM

  // subscribe to external signal — auto-released on destroy
  onMount() {
    this.haunt(externalSignal, (newVal, oldVal) => { ... });
  }

  // manual re-render (prefer signals)
  refresh() { this.render(); }
}
```

`this.ref` — component root DOM element (available in onMount/onRender)
`this.args` — object of values passed from parent via `@:name="expr"`
`this.$abortController` — AbortController used for @on: listeners

---

## Signals

```ts
import { signal, computed, effect, batch } from 'kasper-js';

const count = signal(0);          // writable signal
count.value;                       // read
count.value = 5;                   // write
count.peek();                      // read without tracking dependency

const double = computed(() => count.value * 2);  // derived, read-only

const stop = effect(() => {        // runs immediately, re-runs on change
  console.log(count.value);
});
stop();                            // unsubscribe

batch(() => {                      // flush effects once after all writes
  count.value++;
  count.value++;
});

const unwatch = count.onChange((newVal, oldVal) => { ... });
unwatch();  // unsubscribe

// Arrays — reassignment triggers reactivity; in-place mutation does NOT
items.value = [...items.value, newItem];  // triggers ✓
items.value.push(newItem);               // does NOT trigger ✗
```

---

## Template directives

```html
<!-- Interpolation — only the text node updates -->
<p>{{expression}}</p>

<!-- Conditionals — DOM Boundaries, no wrapper element -->
<div @if="condition">...</div>
<div @elseif="other">...</div>
<div @else>...</div>
<!-- @elseif and @else must be IMMEDIATELY adjacent siblings to @if — no elements between them -->
<!-- @if and @each are independent directives; they cannot be on the same element -->

<!-- Lists — unkeyed (destroy/recreate) -->
<li @each="item of items.value">{{item.name}}</li>

<!-- Lists — with index -->
<li @each="item with i of items.value">{{i}}: {{item.name}}</li>

<!-- Lists — keyed reconciliation (reuses DOM nodes, use for dynamic lists) -->
<li @each="item of items.value" @key="item.id">{{item.name}}</li>

<!-- While loop -->
<div @while="condition">...</div>

<!-- Local variable — scoped to element and children -->
<div @let="total = price * qty">{{total}}</div>

<!-- Two-way binding — there is no @bind directive -->
<!-- Use @on:input to write back to a signal, @attr to reflect its value -->
<input @on:input="name.value = $event.target.value" @attr="{ value: name.value }" />

<!-- Events -->
<button @on:click="handler()">click</button>
<input @on:input="handle($event)" />       <!-- $event is the native DOM Event -->
<form @on:submit.prevent="submit()">...</form>
<!-- modifiers: .prevent .stop .once .passive .capture -->
<!-- $event is the only implicit variable in event handlers — it holds the raw DOM event -->

<!-- Dynamic attributes — class and style are merged -->
<div @attr="{ class: isActive ? 'active' : '', disabled: isDisabled }"></div>
<div @class="isActive ? 'active' : ''"></div>
<div @style="{ color: textColor }"></div>
<!-- @attr uses setAttribute — reliable for most attributes -->
<!-- Exception: boolean DOM properties (checked, selected) diverge from their attribute after user interaction -->
<!-- For checkboxes, drive state through the signal and let @each recreate the element, or set .checked via @ref -->
<input type="checkbox" @on:change="todo.done.value = $event.target.checked" @attr="{ checked: todo.done.value }" />

<!-- DOM ref — captures element into component property -->
<input @ref="inputEl" />

<!-- Component args -->
<user-card @:name="user.name" @:avatar="user.avatar"></user-card>
```

---

## Slots

```html
<!-- Child component defines slots -->
<template>
  <div class="modal">
    <slot @name="title" />   <!-- named slot -->
    <slot />                  <!-- default slot -->
    <slot @name="actions" />
  </div>
</template>

<!-- Parent fills slots -->
<modal>
  <h2 @slot="title">Hello</h2>
  <p>Content goes to default slot.</p>
  <button @slot="actions">OK</button>
</modal>
```

---

## Router

```html
<router>
  <route @path="/" @component="HomePage" />
  <route @path="/users/:id" @component="UserPage" />
  <route @path="/login" @component="LoginPage" />

  <guard @check="checkAuth">
    <route @path="/dashboard" @component="DashboardPage" />
    <route @path="/settings" @component="SettingsPage" />

    <guard @check="checkAdmin">
      <route @path="/admin" @component="AdminPage" />
    </guard>
  </guard>

  <route @path="*" @component="NotFound" />
</router>
```

```ts
import { navigate } from 'kasper-js';
navigate('/dashboard');

// Route params
export class UserPage extends Component {
  onMount() {
    const { id } = this.args.params;
  }
}

// Guard — async, returns Promise<boolean>
async function checkAuth(): Promise<boolean> {
  const user = await fetchCurrentUser();
  if (!user) { navigate('/login'); return false; }
  return true;
}
```

`<route>` and `<guard>` are config nodes — they never render themselves.
Routes matched in order. Use `@path="*"` last as catch-all.
All components referenced in `@component` must be imported in the script block.

---

## State management

Signals are plain ES module exports — no store class needed:

```ts
// store/user.ts
import { signal, computed } from 'kasper-js';

export const currentUser = signal<User | null>(null);
export const isLoggedIn = computed(() => currentUser.value !== null);

export async function login(credentials) {
  currentUser.value = await api.login(credentials);
}
```

```ts
// Navbar.kasper
import { Component } from 'kasper-js';
import { currentUser, isLoggedIn } from '../store/user';

export class Navbar extends Component {
  user = currentUser;       // template reads user.value
  loggedIn = isLoggedIn;

  onMount() {
    this.haunt(currentUser, (user) => {
      // runs on change, auto-cleaned up on destroy
    });
  }
}
```

---

## Vite setup

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import kasper from 'vite-plugin-kasper';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [kasper()],
});
```

```ts
// src/kasper.d.ts — TypeScript declaration for .kasper imports
declare module '*.kasper' {
  import type { Component } from 'kasper-js';
  type AnyComponent = new (...args: any[]) => Component;
  const exports: Record<string, AnyComponent>;
  export = exports;
}
```

---

## Key rules

- One component per `.kasper` file
- All components used in `@component` or as tags must be imported in the script block
- `@on:` listeners are cleaned up automatically on destroy via AbortController
- `haunt()` subscriptions are cleaned up automatically on destroy
- `onRender` fires on every render — do not use for one-time setup, use `onMount`
- Prefer signals for reactivity over calling `this.render()`
- `<style>` blocks are global — prefix class names to avoid collisions
- Use `@key` on `@each` whenever the list can change order or be partially updated
